arguments.task: Open the official Threads developer documentation and gather evidence from official first-party pages only where possible. Find and extract: 1) one-line product/API description, 2) authentication methods used by the Threads API and any setup requirements, 3) access/self-serve vs gated requirements and any need for app review or permissions approval, 4) API surface type and breadth from docs/navigation, 5) rate limit notes, 6) links to official docs pages used. Then separately check whether there is an official or widely recognized MCP server/registry entry for Threads or Meta Threads, preferring official GitHub/MCP registry pages if available. Return a concise structured summary with exact source URLs.

input.thought: Use the browser tool to inspect official Threads documentation and related first-party pages for verifiable research evidence.

raw_result.description: Meta’s Threads API lets developers publish posts, fetch mentions and replies, and access profile and post insights for Threads accounts.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs indicate Meta app setup with App ID/App Secret and user access tokens/permissions for Threads API access; OAuth is used for user authorization, but exact token exchange details were not fully verified in the gathered evidence, so token handling is marked as additional auth mechanism.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public docs are self-serve to read, but production use appears to require specific Threads permissions and App Review approval for at least some scopes such as content publishing and insights-related access.

api_surface.breadth: Threads API is exposed through Meta’s Graph/HTTP endpoints and documented for posting threads, managing replies/mentions, retrieving profiles/media, and reading insights/rate-limit information.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official or clearly verified MCP server evidence was gathered from official sources during the browser run; treat MCP availability as unverified and review manually if needed.

raw_result.blocker: Toolkit development appears feasible from public docs, but app-review and permission approval requirements likely gate full production functionality.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official overview documentation indicates rate limits are enforced and described with a formula-based usage model plus posting limits; exact numeric ceilings beyond posting-related limits were not fully captured in the gathered evidence and should be verified manually.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official documentation includes Overview, Get Started, Reference, permissions/app-review guidance, and rate-limit information, which are strong signals of a real public developer surface. However, Meta permission review requirements suggest nontrivial launch friction for agent tooling.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some fields remain partially ambiguous from the gathered evidence alone: exact auth breakdown between OAuth and token flows, exact access-tier classification for all endpoints, precise rate-limit numbers, and whether any MCP server exists in official or major registries.
